pattern: h, b, f, ff, l, ll, ss
teachingHint: Your child is learning several new sounds: 'h' says h (a breathy sound), 'b' says b (lips pop apart), 'f' says fff (top teeth on bottom lip). They're also learning that some sounds are written with double letters: 'ff' says fff (same as 'f'). The double letters still make just ONE sound. Help your child spot them: hu-ff → huff. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: h-a-d → Say each sound: h, aaa, d. Blend: had
b-a-t → Say each sound: b, aaa, t. Blend: bat
f-a-n → Say each sound: fff, aaa, n. Blend: fan

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! We can't sound this one out. Look at the word and say 'the'. Try to remember what it looks like.

story: A bat had a big fan. The bat sat and got cool!
